found_by: Phase A seal — filesystem cross-check (the check added AFTER J-SPACE caught me)
finding: PerTaskLeaseManager.is_expired() EXISTS. The scheduler NEVER CALLS IT. grep for expiry references in persistent_scheduler.py = 0. There is NO expired-lease reclamation anywhere in the runtime. The lease TTL is DECORATIVE — it is written into every lock file and enforced by nothing.
consequence: CORRECTED. My first claim -- 'pinned 3 of 4 concurrency slots for most of the run' -- was FALSE. active_leases() already filters expired leases, and all 129 rounds dispatched a full 4/4. Concurrency NEVER degraded. I asserted an impact I had not measured.
why_the_ledger_did_not_see_it: The ledger was PERFECTLY CLEAN: 516 ACQUIRED / 516 DISPATCH_START / 516 RELEASED / 0 RELEASE_FAILED. The hung tasks never reached a terminal state, so no release was ever ATTEMPTED — and a release that is never attempted cannot be recorded as failed. The honest-release fix works. It just cannot see a task that never comes back.
j_space_named_this_first: HJOS flagged 'lease_expiry -> RELEASE_EXPIRED_LEASES' as CONTRADICTED hours ago. I correctly disabled its auto-quarantine on governance grounds — and then never fixed the defect it named. The observer was right. I silenced the alarm and left the fire.
fix: scheduler must reclaim expired leases: on each cycle, scan leases, is_expired() -> terminal TIMED_OUT + release + log LEASE_RECLAIMED_EXPIRED
a task with no terminal state past TTL is TIMED_OUT, never silently held
dispatch must carry a hard timeout so a hung adapter cannot pin a slot forever
the sealer's filesystem cross-check STAYS — it is the only thing that caught this
phase_b: BLOCKED — only an unqualified PASS unlocks it
real_consequence: WORK IS DISPATCHED AND SILENTLY LOST. SOAK-HRF-24, SOAK-HSF-24, SOAK-HSF-31 and SOAK-HASF-116 reached DISPATCH_START and then NOTHING -- no COMPLETE, no FAILED, no timeout, no terminal state, no alarm. The tasks simply vanished. The only evidence they ever existed is a lock file that nothing reclaims. A system that can lose work without recording that it lost work cannot hold a baseline, because its history is silently incomplete.
my_own_overclaim: I claimed slot-pinning without measuring it -- the exact failure mode I spent the day auditing (the claim was wider than the measurement). Caught by my own test asserting the impact.
